behavior_summary: U8P_FOOD::FOOD::look responds to an examine/U8P_FOOD::look event by reading the item's frame (variant) and emitting a short descriptive string appropriate to that variant (one of: 'fish ', 'cooked fish ', 'Torax ribs ', 'cooked Torax ribs ').

U8P_FOOD::FOOD::use handles consuming the food item: it makes the actor/item emit a short feedback bark ('Mmmm...'), destroys the item instance, and calls FeedAvatar with an immediate value (2) to restore a small amount of the avatar's food/health meter.
notable_control_flow: FOOD::look: synchronous sequence - call Item::getFrame -> switch on frame index (0..3) -> push speaker id and selected literal string -> call Item::bark -> suspend/return.
FOOD::use: linear sequence - push speaker id and literal 'Mmmm...' -> call Item::bark, then call Item::destroy on the same item instance, then push constant 2 and call FeedAvatar.
The U8P_FOOD::look handler branches on a small discrete domain (frame indices 0..3) to choose descriptive text; the use handler performs an ordered consume/cleanup then a global effect (FeedAvatar).
open_questions: What exact process id (pid) value is passed as the speaker to Item::bark at runtime (inferred from calling convention but not explicitly named in the disassembly)?
Do the four frame indices map to any additional gameplay differences beyond description text (e.g., differing nutrition, spoilage) or is the frame only cosmetic/textual?

Is the numeric value pushed to FeedAvatar precisely '2' in gameplay units across all contexts (inferred from the immediate pushed), and how does that map to player hunger/HP mechanics in the broader engine?

main_function.name: U8P_FOOD::look
main_function.purpose: Provide an inspect/examine description for a food item by selecting a short text based on the item's frame/variant and emitting it to the viewer.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_FOOD::look/examine) — triggered when a player or actor inspects the food item.

function_map.name: U8P_FOOD::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_FOOD::look handler that chooses and emits a descriptive string based on Item::getFrame().

function_map.name: U8P_FOOD::use
function_map.role: Use/consume handler that barks a feedback string, destroys the item, and calls FeedAvatar to restore nutrition.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return the frame/index identifying the food variant (0..3).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display a short text string from an item/process to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Remove/destroy the item instance from the world (called when consumed).

function_map.name: FeedAvatar
function_map.role: Apply a nutrition/food restore effect to the avatar; called with an immediate amount (inferred 2).

external_calls.purpose: Query the item's frame/variant index to decide which description string to show.
Display/emit the selected descriptive text to the inspecting process/player (speaker id pushed before the string).
Provide immediate player feedback ('Mmmm...') when the item is used/consumed.
Remove the consumed item instance from the world/inventory after use.
Increase the avatar's food/health/hunger meter by a small fixed amount (immediate value 2 observed before call; maps to nutrition restoration).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_FOOD::FOOD::look responds to an examine/U8P_FOOD::look event by reading the item's frame (variant) and emitting a short descriptive string appropriate to that variant (one of: 'fish ', 'cooked fish ', 'Torax ribs ', 'cooked Torax ribs ').
